title: EU UBO transparency directive takes effect in 90 days
body: New UBO transparency directive affects 3 steps in the current KYC process map. Two document requirements change, one new verification step needed. Recommended action: begin mini optimisation cycle within 14 days.

title: First-pass approval rate dipped 7 points — training gap detected
body: First-pass approval rate dipped from 81% to 74% last month. Correlates precisely with 3 new analysts who haven't completed the updated risk assessment template training. Not a process issue — a training issue.

title: Claims processing time up 34% — AI model retraining triggered
body: Claims processing time has risen 34% over 12 weeks, breaching the 2.5-day SLA target. Correlated with a 42% volume increase following the new flood risk coverage launch. AI model confidence scores also declining — retraining cycle recommended.

payload.implication: v3.2 achieves 91.8% accuracy, 2.3% fewer false positives than v3.1

title: Claims Lead needs decision: expand AI auto-approval threshold from £500 to £1,000
body: Raising the auto-approval threshold from £500 to £1,000 would automate 16% more claims, saving ~240 analyst hours per month. Modelled fraud exposure increase is £180K/yr — within acceptable risk parameters per the current risk appetite statement.

title: Claims bias audit complete — no systemic patterns detected

artifact.name: Claims bias audit complete — no systemic patterns detected

payload.completionSummary: Bias audit across 5 claim types and 4 age segments. Variance within 5% across all cells. No systemic approval rate disparities detected. Recommended for quarterly board reporting.

title: FCA issued new AI decision-making guidance for insurance — review required
body: FCA published new guidance on AI-assisted decision-making in financial services. Key requirement: customers must be able to request a human review of any AI-driven claim decision. Our current auto-rejection flow may not meet this standard.

data.subLabel: Explainability requirements affect automated rejection flow

title: FirstFlex activation rate 12% below target — drop-off at parental consent step
body: FirstFlex activation rate is 61% lower than benchmarks for the 13–17 segment. The parental consent step has a 47% drop-off — parents are not completing the ID verification flow. Consent flow redesign or alternative verification method recommended.

body: Customer satisfaction trend reversal detected. Q4 2025 NPS declined from 54 to 51. Correlates with delayed feature releases and support ticket volume increase.

title: First-pass approval rate jumps to 81% — pilot success
body: 12-week KYC optimisation pilot delivered exceptional results. First-pass approval rate improved from 42% to 81% — a 39-point gain. New risk assessment template and decision tree logic driving the improvement.

body: Platform stability remains excellent. February 2026 uptime 99.97% — consistent with target. Zero P1 incidents, two minor latency spikes resolved within SLA.

body: Current engineering capacity split: 42% product features, 28% technical debt, 18% platform stability, 12% innovation experiments. Product feature allocation down 8 points from Q4 due to migration support.

body: Claims automation system handling increasing volume. January 2026: 12,400 claims processed, up 18% month-over-month. Auto-approval rate holding at 67%.

body: Embedded finance API adoption accelerating. 47 active partners in January 2026, up from 12 in launch month (July 2025). PayGrid integration represents 38% of API volume.

body: Leadership team must decide H2 2026 investment sequencing across three competing priorities: PayGrid partner expansion, FirstFlex market entry acceleration, or Core Banking migration Phase 3. Each option has strong business case but resource constraints require prioritisation.

options: FirstFlex market entry acceleration (DE + NL launch)

title: AI auto-approval threshold increased to £1,000
body: Leadership approved increasing AI claims auto-approval threshold from £500 to £1,000 effective March 1. Based on 12-week pilot data showing 94% accuracy at higher threshold. Expected to auto-approve additional 2,100 claims per month.

body: Excited to welcome Dr. Sarah Kim to the AI Claims Automation team starting March 1. Sarah joins from TechCorp where she led predictive fraud detection systems. PhD in Machine Learning from MIT. Will focus on improving claims classification accuracy and expanding auto-approval capabilities.

body: Phase 2 risk assessment workshop underway. Reviewing data integrity protocols, rollback procedures, and customer communication timing. Critical path decisions needed on backup validation frequency.

body: Design sprint for improved parental consent flow. Exploring frictionless approval methods while maintaining GDPR compliance for under-16 users. Early concepts showing 40% reduction in abandonment.

Proposal: expand to 3x GPU capacity enabling daily retraining — projected to improve auto-approval accuracy 4-6 points. ROI payback: 7 months via reduced manual review costs.
